# Claude Managed Agents on Blaxel (self-hosted sandbox cookbook)

> Anthropic hosts the CMA agent loop and environment work queue. Blaxel sandboxes run the self-hosted tool execution. This repo uses an orchestrator sandbox for the webhook control plane and one worker sandbox per session for the compute plane. AI agents should read AGENTS.md first.

## Core concepts

- CMA: Anthropic runs Claude, session state, event history, skills, and tool selection.
- Self-hosted execution: tool runtime, filesystem, processes, and network boundary live in the user's infrastructure.
- Blaxel orchestrator: FastAPI webhook on a public preview URL; verifies `session.status_run_started` and starts a worker.
- Blaxel worker: `ant beta:worker poll`; claims the session work, runs tools in `/workspace`, posts results, and exits after `--max-idle`.
- Worker image: self-hosted Docker image with `sandbox-api`, `ant`, and the language runtimes, package managers, database clients, and utilities from Anthropic's cloud sandbox reference. It smokes documented runtime floors, but it is not Anthropic's managed Ubuntu image.

## Credentials

- `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY`: local control-plane key. Creates environments, agents, sessions, and reads events. Never put it on the worker.
- `ANTHROPIC_ENVIRONMENT_KEY`: scoped, revocable environment key. The worker uses it to poll the queue. Agent-run shell can read worker env vars.
- `ANTHROPIC_WEBHOOK_SIGNING_KEY`: `whsec_...` secret used by the orchestrator to verify Anthropic webhooks.
- `BL_API_KEY` and `BL_WORKSPACE`: Blaxel service-account auth for the orchestrator to spawn workers.

## Canonical flow

1. `python3 scripts/preflight.py`
2. `python3 scripts/create_environment.py` -> `ANTHROPIC_ENVIRONMENT_ID`
3. Generate `ANTHROPIC_ENVIRONMENT_KEY` in the Anthropic Console.
4. `(cd worker && bl push --workspace "$BL_WORKSPACE" --type sandbox)`
5. `python3 scripts/create_agent.py` -> `ANTHROPIC_AGENT_ID`
6. `python3 example/run_session.py --local-worker`
7. `(cd orchestrator && bl push --workspace "$BL_WORKSPACE" --type sandbox)`
8. `python3 setup.py`, register webhook, export `ANTHROPIC_WEBHOOK_SIGNING_KEY`, rerun `python3 setup.py`
9. `python3 example/run_session.py`

## Key files

- [README.md](README.md): shortest runnable path.
- [GUIDE.md](GUIDE.md): narrative source for an integration guide.
- [AGENTS.md](AGENTS.md): agent orientation.
- [scripts/](scripts): setup helpers; create scripts print exports and do not mutate `.env`.
- [orchestrator/app.py](orchestrator/app.py): webhook verify, worker spawn, duplicate suppression, poller launch.
- [worker/Dockerfile](worker/Dockerfile): agent runtime image.
- [worker/smoke.sh](worker/smoke.sh): command-surface and runtime-version smoke test for the worker image.
- [example/run_session.py](example/run_session.py): E2E example; `--local-worker` skips webhook.
- [tests/](tests): local tests.

## Invariants

- File tools use relative paths under `/workspace`; bash is still a shell inside the container.
- Tool results must be non-empty.
- Poller uses `keep_alive: True` and a timeout cap.
- `--max-idle` controls poller exit after queue idle.
- `BLAXEL_WORKER_TTL` is max age from sandbox creation, not idle deletion.

## Links

- Anthropic self-hosted sandboxes: https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/managed-agents/self-hosted-sandboxes
- Anthropic cloud sandbox reference: https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/managed-agents/cloud-sandboxes-reference
- Anthropic Managed Agents overview: https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/managed-agents/overview
- Blaxel docs: https://docs.blaxel.ai
